$comment: Canonical Nostr wire contract between ndisc (publisher) and the glmps web viewers (consumers). FROZEN — see schema/README.md. Source of truth: xjmzx/ndisc src-tauri/src/lib.rs (release_event / delete_release); the contract test `mod schema_v1` pins ndisc's output to this file.

release.content: Release notes — plain text. May be an empty string.

d.note: The ONLY structurally guaranteed tag. The `disco-vault:` prefix is the legacy app name and is frozen — do not change it to `ndisc:`.

medium.note: OPTIONAL — frequently absent. Consumers must not require it.

condition.note: Discogs grade verbatim, e.g. 'Near Mint (NM or M-)'.

source.note: URL — emitted only when it is http(s).

release.rule: Every tag except `d` is omitted entirely when its value is empty. Consumers MUST require only `d` structurally and treat all other tags — including `title`, `artist`, and `medium` — as optional.

a.note: Addressable coordinate of the deleted release. Authoritative — match this verbatim.

k.note: Redundant: the kind is already encoded in `a`. Consumers may ignore `k`.

deletion.rule: Consumers MUST apply deletions client-side. Some relays (e.g. Primal) do not enforce kind:5, so server-side filtering cannot be relied on.

profile.note: ndisc reads the author's kind:0 metadata (name / nip05 / picture) for display. It never publishes kind:0.

changePolicy: FROZEN. Any change to the emitted event format is a coordinated v2 bump — add release.v2.json and migrate consumers — never an edit to this file.
